metadata.description: Intentionally failing test case. Moves leadscrew to 100mm then checks for a position in the 150–200mm range — physically impossible for this leadscrew (max travel 720mm but the commanded position is 100mm, so the channel will never report 150+). The channel_wait step times out and is reported as TEST_FAIL. This validates that the testrig correctly detects and reports when firmware does not reach a commanded position, and that on_fail recovery commands execute after a test failure.

steps.description: Command leadscrew to 100mm — this is a valid, reachable position
Wait for position to reach 100mm setpoint before the bad check

INTENTIONAL FAIL: check for position in 150–200mm range. The leadscrew was commanded to 100mm and will never reach 150mm, so this step will timeout. Expected result: TEST_FAIL on this step. This confirms the testrig correctly detects when the firmware does not reach a target position.
Return to home after the failed check — stop_if_fail=false on step 4 means this still executes
